title: vandellia khaoyaiensis
intro: Vandellia khaoyaiensis is a plant species belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the vandellia or water hawthorn. Traditionally, this plant is used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Vandellia khaoyaiensis are said to include wound healing, anti-aging, and the treatment of various health conditions such as fever and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
